examples.title: Basic textarea
examples.description: Multi-line text input with label and bound value.

examples.title: Disabled textarea
examples.description: A disabled textarea is dimmed and non-editable; its value is still shown so context is preserved while editing is locked.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Textarea } from '@lostgradient/cinder/textarea';

</script>

<Textarea
  id="field"
  value="This account note is read-only and managed by your administrator."
  label="Account note"
  description="Contact an administrator to change this."
  disabled
/>

examples.title: Required textarea
examples.description: Setting `required` forwards the native `required` attribute, which browsers expose as `aria-required="true"` and enforce during form submission.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Textarea } from '@lostgradient/cinder/textarea';

  let message = $state('');
</script>

<Textarea
  id="field"
  bind:value={message}
  label="Message"
  description="This field is required."
  required
/>

examples.title: Live character counter
examples.description: Set `showCount` together with `maxlength` to render a live `{used}/{limit}` counter below the field that updates as the user types.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Textarea } from '@lostgradient/cinder/textarea';

  let summary = $state('A short product summary.');
</script>

<Textarea
  id="field"
  bind:value={summary}
  label="Summary"
  description="Keep it under 200 characters."
  showCount
  maxlength={200}
/>

examples.title: Textarea with error
examples.description: Validation error wired via aria-invalid and aria-describedby.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Textarea } from '@lostgradient/cinder/textarea';
  let message = $state('');
  const hasError = $derived(message.length > 0 && message.length < 20);
</script>

<Textarea
  id="message"
  bind:value={message}
  label="Message"
  rows={5}
  placeholder="Write at least 20 characters…"
  {...hasError ? { error: 'Minimum 20 characters.' } : {}}
/>
